title: Notes from Underground
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
content: “I am a sick man... I am a spiteful man. I am an unattractive man. I believe my liver is diseased. However, I know nothing at all about my disease, and do not know for certain what part of me is affected.”
action: Acknowledge the struggle of diagnosing your own internal failures.

title: The Iliad
author: Homer
content: “Like the generations of leaves, the lives of mortal men. Now the wind scatters the old leaves across the earth, now the living timber bursts with the new buds and spring comes round again.”
action: Accept the cyclical nature of renewal and replacement in your systems.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
content: “When we are no longer able to change a situation, we are challenged to change ourselves.”
action: Adapt your internal mindset when external agency is exhausted.

title: Paradise Lost
author: John Milton
content: “The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven.”
action: Assert cognitive sovereignty over your working environment.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
content: “Too much sanity may be madness—and maddest of all: to see life as it is, and not as it should be!”
action: Balance objective reality with the necessity of a guiding ideal.
